put.description: Same effect as `addHistoryItem` with `AddTimeOffHistoryOverrideRequest` except that each adjustement is limited to -24 to 24 hours.

location.description: API URL for the new time off history item.

get.description: The results will only contain time off types that the API user is authorized to see.

put.description:
Adds a time-off history item to accrue or use time-off on a given date.
The history item can be for used time-off which is associated with a time-off request.  This is added using `AddTimeOffHistoryItemRequest` and documented as [Add a time off history entry](https://www.bamboohr.com/api/documentation/time_off.php#addHistoryEntry).
Alternatively the history item can be an override which is not associated with a time-off request.  This is added using `AddTimeOffHistoryOverrideRequest` and documented as [Add a time off history override](https://www.bamboohr.com/api/documentation/time_off.php#addHistoryOverride).  This has the same effect as the `adjustTimeOffBalance` operation without the restriction for hours to be between -24 and 24.

get.description: Use this resource to request one of your pre-existing company reports from the reporting section. You can get the report number by hovering over the report name on the reports page and grabbing the ID. At present, only reports from the Company Reports section are supported. In the future we may implement reports from the Report Library section if there is enough demand for it. The report numbers used in this request are different in each company.

get.summary: Request a company report in a specified file format
get.description: Use this resource to request one of your pre-existing company reports from the reporting section. You can get the report number by hovering over the report name on the reports page and grabbing the ID. At present, only reports from the Company Reports section are supported. In the future we may implement reports from the Report Library section if there is enough demand for it. The report numbers used in this request are different in each company.

post.description: Use this resource to request BambooHR generate a report. The custom report will return employees regardless of their status, "Active" or "Inactive". This differs from the UI, which by default applies a quick filter to display only "Active" employees.

post.summary: Request a custom report in a specified file format
post.description: Use this resource to request BambooHR generate a report. The custom report will return employees regardless of their status, "Active" or "Inactive". This differs from the UI, which by default applies a quick filter to display only "Active" employees.

400.description: If any required field is missing, any of the ids are invalid, or the posted JSON is not valid.
If any required field is missing, any of the ids are invalid, or the posted JSON is not valid.
Time tracking id cannot be found.
If any required field is missing, any of the ids are invalid, or the posted JSON is not valid.

get.description:
The response will be limited to those employees and time off types that the owner of the API key used to make the request has view access to.

When using start and/or end dates the time off will show up if any day in the time off request falls within the dates specified, even if part of time off falls outside of the time specified.

Clients should be ready for any number of "note" elements.

If there are zero notes for a request then the "notes" tag will not be included in the response.

Newer time off requests may also include details for each day of the request in a <dates> element.

put.description:
Any email alerts that would normally be triggered by the web UI will be triggered by the use of this API.

The note provided will be used as the manager's note if the status is approved or denied.

403.description: User doesn't have access to change the status in this way.

404.description: The time off request ID doesn't exist.

get.summary: Get a list of who's out, including company holidays
get.description:
This API will return a list, sorted by date, of employees who will be out, and company holidays, for a period of time.

The results may contain time off elements for which the API user cannot request details.

apiKey.description:
Requests to the BambooHR API are authenticated by sending an "API Key" for the user to be authenticated as the HTTP Basic username.  The password is ignored, but is commonly shown as "x" in the documentation.

See https://documentation.bamboohr.com/docs#section-authentication

changeType.name: type
changeType.description: Limit changes to a given type

employeeFields.name: fields
employeeFields.description:
Employee fields to return in the response.
Note: The id field is always returned.

employeeIdFilter.name: employeeid
employeeIdFilter.description: Limit response to a given Employee ID

endDate.name: end
endDate.description: End date of (inclusive) date range to include

filterDuplicates.name: fd
filterDuplicates.description: Apply standard duplicate field filtering?  Default yes

hashedEmail.name: h
hashedEmail.description:
The hash of the employee's work email. If they don't have a work email then the hash of the home email will work. If they do have a work email then the home email will not work.

The email address must be representable in ASCII.

The hash is computed as the following md5(strtolower(trim($email))), where the trim() function eliminates ASCII whitespace, the strtolower() function converts ASCII text to lowercase and the md5 function produces a hex string representing the MD5 hash of the data. In PHP you can use these exact functions, in other languages there will be equivalent functions you can use.

precision.name: precision
precision.description: Number of digits after the decimal point to include.

since.name: since
since.description: Get changes since a given date

startDate.name: start
startDate.description: Start date of (inclusive) date range to include

timeOffRequestAction.name: action
timeOffRequestAction.description: Limit to requests that the user has a particular level of access to.

timeOffRequestEmployeeId.name: employeeId
timeOffRequestEmployeeId.description: Limit response to a given employee ID

timeOffRequestIdQuery.name: id
timeOffRequestIdQuery.description: Limit response to a given time off request ID

timeOffRequestStatus.name: status
timeOffRequestStatus.description: Time off request statuses to include

timeOffTypesMode.name: mode
timeOffTypesMode.description: Set to "request" to get a list of all time off types with which this user can create a time off request. The default is to return the list of time off types the user has permissions on. This distinction is important, as employees can request time off for types that they don't have permission to view balances and requests for.

whoIsOutEndDate.name: end
whoIsOutEndDate.description: End of the date range to consider. Defaults to 14 days from the start date.

whoIsOutStartDate.name: start
whoIsOutStartDate.description: Start of the date range to consider. Defaults to current date.

CurrencyCode.description: ISO 4217 currency code supported by BambooHR

CurrencyAmountString.description:
String representing an amount of a particular currency.
Note: In some fields the number can be omitted (e.g. in bonusAmount) presumably this should be interpreted as 0.

EmployeeFieldName.description: Property names of the Employee schema.

CompensationChangeReason.description: Compensation Change Reason list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API.

enum: Congo, the Democratic Republic of the
South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

CountryCode.description:
2-letter country codes recognized by BambooHR.

Based on ISO 3166-2, with some previously removed entries:
- AN for Netherlands Antilles
- FX for France, Metropolitan
- YU for Yugoslavia
- ZR for Zaire

And some non-standard additions:
- XK for Kosovo

Division.description: Division list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API.

EEOJobCategory.description: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission Job Categories https://www.eeoc.gov/employers/eeo1survey/jobclassguide.cfm

enum: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

JobTitle.description: Job Title list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API.

Location.description: Location list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API.

PayGroup.description: Pay Groups list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API. https://{company}.bamboohr.com/settings/list.php?id=pay_group

PaySchedule.description: Pay schedules, as defined in Employee Fields Settings https://{company}.bamboohr.com/settings/pay_schedules

ProvinceCode.description:
Canadian province codes recognized by BambooHR.

Matches postal abbreviations for states and possessions, excluding military abbreviations (AA, AE, AP)

ProvinceName.description: Canadian province names recognized by BambooHR.

StateCode.description:
State codes recognized by BambooHR.

Matches postal abbreviations for states and possessions, excluding military abbreviations (AA, AE, AP)

TerminationReason.description: Termination Reason list values, as defined in Employee Fields Settings and Lists in the Metadata API.

RgbColor.description: String representation of a 24-bit RGB (aka true color) color value.

MonthDay.description: String representation of a 2-digit month and 2-digit day.

age.description:
The employee's age. To change age, update dateOfBirth field.
Has value -1 when dateOfBirth is unspecified.

bestEmail.description: The employee's work email if set, otherwise their home email.

birthday.description:
The employee's month and day of birth. To change birthday, update dateOfBirth field.
Is empty string if dateOfBirth is unspecified.

dateOfBirth.description: The date the employee was born.

employmentHistoryStatus.description: The employee's CURRENT employment status. Options are customized by account. Read-only starting with version 1.1; update using the employmentStatus table.

fullName1.description: The employee's first and last name. (e.g., John Doe).

fullName2.description: The employee's last and first name. (e.g., Doe, John).

fullName3.description: The employee's full name and their preferred name. (e.g., Doe, John Quentin (JDog)).

fullName4.description: The employee's full name without their preferred name, last name first. (e.g., Doe, John Quentin).

fullName5.description: The employee's full name without their preferred name, first name first. (e.g., John Quentin Doe).

displayName.description: The employee's name displayed in a format configured by the user.

hireDate.description: The date the employee was hired.

originalHireDate.description:
The date the employee was originally hired. Available starting with version 1.1.

Note: Attempting to update to "0000-00-00" has no effect. Use "" or null to clear.

id.description: The employee ID automatically assigned by BambooHR.

jobTitle.description: The CURRENT value of the employee's job title, updating this field will create a new row in position history.

lastChanged.description: The date and time that the employee record was last changed.

payChangeReason.description: The reason for the employee's last pay rate change.

payGroup.description: The custom pay group that the employee belongs to.

payGroupId.description: The ID value corresponding to the pay group that an employee belongs to.

payRateEffectiveDate.description: The day the most recent change was made.

payScheduleId.description: The ID value corresponding to the pay schedule that an employee belongs to.

includeInPayroll.description: Should employee be included in payroll

timeTrackingEnabled.description: Should time tracking be enabled for the employee

sin.description: The employee's Canadian Social Insurance Number.

state.description:
In responses from getEmployee:  The employee's state code (if USA), province (if Canada), or state/province name.

In responses from getReport and getCustomReport: The employee's state/province name.

stateCode.description: The 2 character abbreviation for the employee's state (US only).

supervisor.description: The employee’s CURRENT supervisor as last name-comma-first name-space-middle name.

supervisorId.description: The 'employeeNumber' of the employee's CURRENT supervisor.

supervisorEId.description: The ID of the employee's CURRENT supervisor.

terminationDate.description: The date the employee was terminated. Read-only starting with version 1.1; update using the employmentStatus table.

workPhone.description: The employee's work phone number, without extension.

workPhonePlusExtension.description: The employee's work phone and extension.

workPhoneExtension.description: The employee's work phone extension (if any).

isPhotoUploaded.description: Whether a photo has been uploaded for the employee.

acaStatus.description: The employee's ACA (Affordable Care Act) Full-Time status.

acaStatusCategory.description: The employee's ACA (Affordable Care Act) status.

standardHoursPerWeek.description: The number of hours the employee works in a standard week.

bonusDate.description: The date of the last bonus.

bonusAmount.description: The amount of the most recent bonus

bonusReason.description: The reason for the most recent bonus.

bonusComment.description: Comment about the most recent bonus.

commissionDate.description: The date of the last commission.

commisionDate.description: This field name contains a typo, and exists for backwards compatibility.

commissionAmount.description: The amount of the most recent commission.

commissionComment.description: Comment about the most recent commission.

canUploadPhoto.description:
The employee is permitted to upload a photo.
This field is only included in getDirectory responses.

photoUploaded.description: The employee has uploaded a photo.

photoUrl.description: Employee photo url, or a placeholder photo URL when photoUploaded is false.

facebook.description: The employee's Facebook username or URL

twitterFeed.description: The employee's Twitter username or URL

fields.description:
Employee fields to include in the custom report.
Note: If fields is empty or not specified, only one employee is returned.  To avoid this unexpected behavior, it is specified here as required with minLength: 1

size.description: Size of the file content, in bytes.

createdBy.description: Full name of the user who uploaded the file.

category.description: Category ID in which to place the uploaded file.

file.description:
Photo to upload.

- The width and height of the photo must be the same number of pixels.
- The source photo must be a JPEG, GIF, or PNG.
- The photo file may not be larger than 20MB.

state.description: State code, province code, or province name (if code unavailable) of dependent's address
State code, province code, or province name (if code unavailable) of emergency contact's address

date.description:
Date on which the employee began working this job.
Note: Invalid date values in requests are ignored.

location.description:
Location where the employee worked for this job.
Responses contain an empty string, if no other value is specified.
Note: Values which do not match a `name` from the "Location" list (case insensitively, ignoring trailing spaces) are ignored.

division.description:
Division in which the employee worked for this job.
Responses contain an empty string, if no other value is specified.
Note: Values which do not match a `name` from the "Division" list (case insensitively, ignoring trailing spaces) are ignored.

department.description:
Department in which the employee worked for this job.
Responses contain an empty string, if no other value is specified.
Note: Values which do not match a `name` from the "Department" list (case insensitively, ignoring trailing spaces) are ignored.

jobTitle.description:
Job title for the employee during this job.
Responses contain an empty string, if no other value is specified.
Note: Values which do not match a `name` from the "Job Title" list (case insensitively, ignoring trailing spaces) are ignored.

reportsTo.description:
Name of the employee's supervisor for this job.
Responses use value from `fullName1` employee field, or an empty string if not specified.
Note: Values which do not match `fullName1`, `fullName2`, `fullName4`, or `fullName5` for a current employee (case insensitively, ignoring trailing spaces) are ignored.

x-enum-descriptions: Available time-off is automatically accrued at a defined rate.
Available time-off is at the discretion of the employee (i.e. unlimited)

TimeOffRequestNote.description: A note made on a time off request.

TimeOffRequestNotes.description: Earliest note made on a time off request by the employee and manager. If either has made multiple notes, only the first is returned.

TimeOffTypeId.description: Time off type identifier defined in the Metadata API https://www.bamboohr.com/api/documentation/metadata.php#timeOffTypes and visible in Settings > Time Off in the web UI.

dates.description: Maps ISO date string to number of time-off hours requested.

notes.description: Earliest note made on a time off request by the employee and manager. If either has made multiple notes, only the first is returned. If neither have made notes, an empty array is returned.

start.description: Although dates in the past are accepted, the request will not appear in the "Upcoming Time Off" section of the "Time Off" tab and therefore may be difficult to approve.

amount.description:
The number of days/hours to update the employee's balance by. The unit will be whatever the time off type is configured for.
The number may be positive, negative, or zero.  (Note: This author does not know in what cases negative values might be requested.)

notes.description:
Notes/Comments from the employee and/or manager about this request.
If multiple notes from the same source are included, all but the last are currently ignored.

dates.description:
The <dates></dates> section is optional. If you don't provide it then all of the time off for the request will be deducted on the first day of the request.

If you do provide details for the dates, then the amount for the overall request is ignored and the total amount of time requested is simply the sum of the amounts for each day.

Keys are ISO date strings, values are number of hours requested.

previousRequest.description: If you supply the "previousRequest" parameter with a valid "time off request ID" then the original request will be canceled and the new request will be marked as its successor. This allows you to change a request that has already been approved. A request can be changed any time before the start date of the time off. The new request must list the same employee ID as the original request.

AddTimeOffHistoryRequest.description: Base type for `addHistoryItem` request body. **Do not use this class directly.  Use a subclass.**

date.description: By convention, the date of the history item should be the first date of the time off range.

note.description: Note attached to the history item.

timeOffRequestId.description: Time off request this history item is linked to.

amount.description:
The number of days/hours of requested time off.
The unit will be whatever the time off type is configured for.
May be positive (to accrue), negative (to use), or zero.
Is not limited to 24 hours, unlike `AdjustTimeOffBalanceRequest`.

date.description: The date the adjustment should be added in history.

timeOffTypeId.description: The ID of the time off type to add a balance adjustment for.

amount.description:
The number of hours by which to adjust the balance.
Although adjustments are limited to +/-24, the sum of adjustment amounts on the same day is not limited.

note.description: This is an optional note to show in history.

accrualStartDate.description:
Date on which this policy starts.
null removes the assignment, except for the "BambooHR Unlimited Policy" type (shown as "None" in the UI), which is the default.
Must be _after_ employee `hireDate` or 400 "There is already a policy assigned for that time off type" will be returned.

name.description:
Holiday name if type is "holiday".
Employee name if type is "timeOff".

employeeId.description: 0 if user is not linked to an employee

Users.description: Map from user ID to user.

employees.description: Map from employee ID to change information for the employee.

applicationKey.description: The application key provided to you by BambooHR.

deviceId.description: An ID for the user's mobile device. This deviceId can be generated when the app is first installed on a device, and should be unique enough to prevent collisions between users within the same company.

state.description:
In responses, State/Province name, or `false` if not specified.
In request body, State/Province code.

country.description:
In responses, the country name.
In requests, 2-character ISO country code.

jobData.description: A list of up to four 20 characters max job numbers in comma delimited format with no spaces

schema.description: Unused type for CSV report content
Currently used type for CSV report content

tags.name: Benefits
tags.description:
There are two primary entities when dealing with benefits: Benefit Plans and Benefit Groups.

Benefit Plans define the Type (medical, dental, etc.), Coverage options (employee, employee + spouse, etc.), and Total Monthly Costs (i.e. the premium). Each plan will also show when it went into effect and when it is set to expire. Plans must be configured before you can create Benefit Groups.

Benefit Groups allow you to create a customizable benefit package for specific employee groupings. For example, you may want to have a Benefit Group for all of your full time employees for which you include a Medical, Dental, Vision, and 401K plan. You could then have a separate Benefit Group for your part time employees which allows for Medical only. Plan eligibility rules and group-specific employee pays and company pays amounts are set in benefit groups (with the exception of plans where pays information is set at the employee level).
In addition to getting benefit information through the API, you can also get employee deductions for a given plan and define the dependents associated with your employees. You cannot currently enroll employees or change benefit status through the API.

tags.name: Changes
tags.description:
The Last Changed API allows you to discover which employees have been recently added, changed, or deleted. Any change to any field inside an employee record will count as an update. Insert events are only recorded when an employee record is first created. Delete events are only recorded when an employee record is deleted.

**Note:** Last change timestamps are a newer feature in BambooHR. We started recording these dates on June 5th, 2011. We do not have last changed data for any changes that occurred before this date.

tags.name: Employees
tags.description: Operations for adding and retrieving employee information, and associated files.

tags.name: Login
tags.description:
Access control to the API is always controlled by an API key that identifies a particular user. Usually, this API key is generated on the website and then copied into your third party application. It is now possible for registered applications to generate new API keys for each user on the fly by making a special API request.
**Note:** Each application using this approach is required to provide an application key to identify what application is requesting the API key. For more information on obtaining application keys please contact support@bamboohr.com.
**Note:** If you already have an API key, you do not need to use the Login API.

tags.name: Metadata
tags.description:
The Metadata API allows you to retrieve "data about data" for a given BambooHR account. You can determine what fields a customer is using. You can determine what options are available in a list. You can determine what users are able to access the system.
Metadata is important because BambooHR is highly configurable. A given customer's account may have drastically different settings from another customer. Using this API, you can determine what values the account supports.
Read only access to metadata does not require any special permissions. When write access to metadata is supported then the user will need the appropriate permissions to create new users, for example.

tags.name: Payroll
tags.description:
There are four primary entities used in building the BambooHR paystub experience:
- Raw paystub data - Pay period dates (past, current, and future) - Direct deposit settings - Tax withholding settings
All of the information displayed as part of the paystub experience is read-only, although employees may navigate through paystub history, view specific paystub details, and then download or print the paystub.
Raw paystub data (income, withholdings, deductions, etc.) and pay period data (past, present, and future) are required to generate the primary paystub experience in BambooHR. Direct deposit and tax withholding data completes the experience for the employee, but is not critical. If no direct deposit or tax withholding data are provided, those portions of the experience will not be displayed to the employee user.

tags.name: Photos
tags.description: The Photos API allows you to retrieve employee photos. There is a standard API-based mechanism for retrieving the photo binary data.

tags.name: Tables
tags.description: Some data in the BambooHR system is stored as a two dimensional grid for each employee. The tables API allows you to grab tabular data for the various tables in BambooHR. For standard tables, a series of fields will be outlined below. API support for custom tables will be added in a future release.

tags.name: TimeOff
tags.description:
There are two primary entities when dealing with time off. The requests and the history.
Requests represent a request from an employee to take time off. A request needs to be approved before it will be recorded into the history. A user with sufficient permissions can create a request and mark it approved at the same time.
The history is log of all events that effect an employee's balance. The current balance of time off is the sum of all events in the history. Time off used decreases the total, while time off accruals increase it.

tags.name: TimeTracking
tags.description:
The time tracking API is used to send approved hours worked to BambooHR, similar to how you would send hours to a payroll company. These hours are stored in BambooHR and can be used to accrue paid time off using BambooHR’s accrual feature, and/or sent to a BambooHR payroll partner during the payroll run. This makes it possible for the customer to use BambooHR as the source of all payroll related data.
Time records can be added to BambooHR or can be retrieved from BambooHR. When adding time records to BambooHR, certain fields are required with each entry such as a date, associated pay rate, and a rate type (see documentation below). Identifying information for the employee is also required so the hours may be associated with the proper employee in BambooHR. Lists of employees and their accompanying identifying information can be retrieved through our api (see Single Dimensional Data).
Please note that only approved hours should be sent to BambooHR since there will be no distinction of approved or not-approved in BambooHR. If non-approved hours are sent to BambooHR, accruals may occur on those hours and they also may be passed to payroll. Edits to approved hours (additions or subtractions) should be sent as new records with their associated date (the date of the hours worked).
